elements.name: Uranium
elements.curation_notes: Curated: 4f doublet + 4d family. Nominals are U METAL values, but real samples are almost always oxidized (UCl4, U4+/U6+ oxides) and sit several eV HIGHER — expected_region_ev bands are therefore widened upward through the common oxidation states (curator-directed, 2026-06) so orientation bands cover where actual sample peaks appear. U-4d3/2 nominal is DERIVED (4d5/2 + DS-4d median), not a direct NIST record — hand-check. UO2/U3O8/UO3 4f7/2 chemical-state values were NOT archive-recoverable (compound pages 2-5 unarchived) — uncurated; NIST v4.1 line list confirms 4f7/2,sat / 4f5/2,sat satellite records exist but their values were not recoverable — uncurated. Shallow lines (4s, 5d, 5p3/2, 6p, 6s) are curated from the complete 2004 NIST element page. 4p3/2 exists in NIST's v4.1 line list but no value was archive-recoverable — uncurated. U 5f is NOT tabulated as a line in NIST SRD 20 (valence character) — intentionally absent, not an omission. 5p1/2 is not in NIST's line list — 5p3/2 is curated unpaired (the DS-5p record, 63.6 eV FBWF74, is noted but unused). One NIST compound record (U3O7 4d3/2 = 105.3 eV) is a physically impossible data-entry artifact and was discarded. Asymmetric U 4f broadening in U(IV) compounds arises from 5f multiplet coupling — use multiplet models, not single DS peaks (see CLAUDE.md). Values recovered from NIST SRD 20 v3.5/v4.1 via Internet Archive snapshots (2004/2015/2016/2023).

transitions.notes: Nominal is U METAL: median of the 12-record metal cluster (376.5-377.6: HoTh79, VRPC74, AlTu73, ACCT74, Chad73, MCSS82, WRDM79, FBWF74, DMKK84z, ScLa81, WCSW86, WCSW87). Real samples are usually oxidized (UCl4, U4+/U6+) and appear SEVERAL eV HIGHER — the region spans the oxidation states: NIST-tabulated points are the metal cluster, the oxidized-surface record 380.2 (ChGr72), and ~380.8 implied by NIST U(VI) uranate 4f5/2 records (391.6, Cs2U2O7/Cs2UO4) minus DS-4f 10.8; top extended to 383.0 for U(VI) oxides (curator-directed margin, 2026-06). Splitting = median of 9 NIST DS-4f records (10.7-10.9); area_ratio 4:3 from 4f degeneracy.

Nominal is U METAL: direct NIST record (WCSW87); cross-check 377.6 (WCSW87 4f7/2) + 10.8 = 388.4 OK. Real samples are usually oxidized and appear higher — region spans NIST-tabulated oxidized states: D2O/U 388.0-391.5, Cs2U2O7 / Cs2UO4 uranates 391.6; top 393.8 mirrors the 4f7/2 band top (383.0) + DS-4f (10.8) for doublet congruence (curator-directed margin, 2026-06).

Nominal is U METAL: four NIST records 736.0 (FBWF74), 736.4 (ScLa81), 736.7 (AlTu73), 736.8 (ACCT74) — 736.4 chosen as the record nearest the median; hand-check. Real samples are usually oxidized and appear higher — region spans NIST-tabulated oxide records UO2 737.7 / 738.6, U3O7 739.5; top extended to 742.0 for U(VI) (curator-directed margin, 2026-06; UO3 4d values not archive-recoverable). Splitting = median of NIST DS-4d records (42.1 x2, 42.3); area_ratio 3:2 from 4d degeneracy.

DERIVED: 736.4 + 42.1 (DS-4d median) = 778.5 — no direct NIST elemental 4d3/2 record was archive-recoverable. Needs hand-verification against live SRD 20. Real samples are usually oxidized and appear higher — region spans the NIST-tabulated oxide record U3O7 781.7; top extended to 784.5 for U(VI) (curator-directed margin, 2026-06).
Single NIST record (FBWF74) — low-confidence nominal. Oxidation-widened fallback region (U metal nominal; real samples shift higher).

Three NIST records: 93.6 (AlTu73), 94.0 (ScLa81 — median record, chosen), 94.2 (FBWF74). DS-5d records DISAGREE: 8.2 (AlTu73) vs 8.6 (FBWF74) — 8.6 chosen for internal consistency with FBWF74's comprehensive U set; HAND-CHECK. Oxidation-widened fallback region; area_ratio 3:2 from 5d degeneracy.

DERIVED: 94.0 + 8.6 (DS-5d, FBWF74) = 102.6 — no direct NIST record archive-recoverable (the 5d3/2 line exists in NIST's v4.1 list). DOUBLE uncertainty: the splitting itself is contested (8.2 vs 8.6). Needs hand-verification.

Single NIST record (FBWF74) — low-confidence nominal. Curated UNPAIRED: NIST's line list has no 5p1/2 entry (the DS-5p record, 63.6 eV FBWF74, is noted but unused). Oxidation-widened fallback region. OVERLAPS the Cl 2p region in actinide chlorides (e.g. UCl4) — expect interference.

Three NIST records: 16.8 (FBWF74), 16.9 (WCSW87 — median, chosen), 18.0 (VeLa74). DS-6p records DISAGREE: 10.0 (FBWF74) vs 10.5 (VeLa74) — 10.0 chosen (internally consistent with FBWF74's 16.8); HAND-CHECK. Oxidation-widened fallback region; area_ratio 2:1 from 6p degeneracy.

DERIVED: 16.9 + 10.0 (DS-6p, FBWF74) = 26.9 — no direct NIST record archive-recoverable (the 6p1/2 line exists in NIST's v4.1 list). Splitting contested (10.0 vs 10.5). Needs hand-verification. Overlaps the O 2s region (~28.3) in oxidized samples.
Single NIST record (FBWF74) — low-confidence nominal. Oxidation-widened fallback region.
